title: Secure first confirmed upstream maintainer partner
context: The project's 'delivered, not merged' bar requires at least one named upstream maintainer who will actively review and accept contributions. The pilot (pilot-007) demonstrates the flow works, but a confirmed partner relationship — where the maintainer has explicitly agreed to review further contributions — is what enables sustainable throughput in M1 and beyond. Depends on outreach-006 (which opened the initial threads); this task converts the best thread into a confirmed partnership.
objective: Secure a named upstream maintainer who confirms they will review and accept contributions for their tool, document the contribution mechanism, and update the affected tasks to verifiedNeed: true with the requestor set.
acceptanceCriteria: A named upstream maintainer confirms (via issue thread, email, or direct communication) that they will review and accept contributions for a specific tool.
The contribution mechanism is documented: PR flow, DCO/CLA requirement, contributing guide URL, CI expectations.
The confirmed partnership is recorded as a committed document with the maintainer's name, tool, confirmation URL/reference, and contribution mechanism.
All tasks targeting that tool are updated to verifiedNeed: true with the requestor field set to the maintainer's name or GitHub handle.
The confirmed partner relationship becomes the primary liaison point for the Steward for M1 and M2 contributions to that tool.
output: A committed record of the first confirmed upstream maintainer partner, with contribution mechanism documented and affected tasks updated to verifiedNeed: true, enabling M1 contributions to proceed with a confirmed acceptance path.
